_comment: Franchise Navigator data file — place at /franchises/final-fantasy.json.
description: Created by Hironobu Sakaguchi in 1987, Final Fantasy is Square Enix's flagship RPG franchise spanning 16 mainline entries, two MMOs, spin-off series, films, anime, manga, and novels. Each installment is a standalone universe united by recurring motifs: crystals, chocobos, moogles, and a character named Cid.

media.title: Final Fantasy
media.description: The game that saved Square from bankruptcy. Multiple remasters; Pixel Remaster (2021) is the recommended version.

media.title: Final Fantasy II
media.description: Action-based leveling system; first Firion and Minwu.

media.title: Final Fantasy III
media.description: Introduced the job system. Western debut via DS remake (2006).

media.title: Final Fantasy IV
media.description: First Active Time Battle system. Cecil's redemption arc. Strong characters and story.
media.notes: DS 3D remake is a worthy second playthrough.

media.title: Final Fantasy V
media.description: Most flexible job system of the classic era. Bartz and Exdeath.

media.title: Final Fantasy VI
media.description: Kefka as the true villain. Opera scene. Ensemble cast. Widely considered the SNES pinnacle.
media.notes: First game directed by Yoshinori Kitase.

media.title: Final Fantasy VII
media.description: Western JRPG breakthrough. Cloud, Aerith, Sephiroth, Midgar. Cultural landmark.
media.notes: Part of the FFVII Compilation universe.

media.title: Final Fantasy VIII
media.description: Junction system; SeeD mercenaries; Squall and Rinoa romance-focused narrative.

media.title: Final Fantasy IX
media.description: Love letter to classic FF. Zidane, Garnet, Vivi. Best story of the PS1 era; most accessible mechanics.

media.title: Final Fantasy X
media.description: First voiced FF. Sphere grid leveling. Zanarkand. Tidus and Yuna. Emotional narrative.
media.notes: HD Remaster (2013) is the recommended version. Includes FFX-2 and the 'Will' audio drama.

media.title: Final Fantasy X-2
media.description: First direct sequel in main series. Dressphere job system. Yuna as protagonist. Divisive but mechanically excellent.

media.title: Final Fantasy XI
media.description: First MMORPG in the series. Servers still active as of 2025. Rich story across multiple expansions.

media.title: Final Fantasy XII
media.description: Gambit AI system. Ivalice setting. The Zodiac Age remaster (2017) is the recommended version.
media.notes: Yasumi Matsuno departed mid-development; Hiroshi Minagawa and Hiroyuki Ito completed the game.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIII
media.description: Linear corridor design; paradigm battle system. Lightning as protagonist. Commercially successful; critically divisive.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIII-2
media.description: Monster taming; time travel; addressed XIII criticism. Serah as protagonist.

media.title: Lightning Returns: Final Fantasy XIII
media.description: Action combat; 13-day countdown structure. Trilogy closer.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIV: A Realm Reborn
media.description: Rebuilt MMO after catastrophic 1.0 launch. Now one of the most-played and beloved MMOs in the world. Ongoing expansion content.
media.notes: Original FFXIV 1.0 launched 2010 and was shut down 2012. ARR launched 2013. Expansions: Heavensward (2015), Stormblood (2017), Shadowbringers (2019), Endwalker (2021), Dawntrail (2024).

media.title: Final Fantasy XV
media.description: Open-world road trip RPG. Noctis and his three companions. 10 years in development. Royal Edition (2018) includes all DLC.
media.notes: Four planned DLC episodes canceled 2018 when director Tabata departed. Stories novelized in The Dawn of the Future.

media.title: Final Fantasy XVI
media.description: Dark political fantasy; action combat system. Eikons (summons) as weapons of war. Rated M. Produced by Naoki Yoshida.

media.title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
media.description: Part 1 of the FFVII Remake trilogy. Midgar as a full 40-hour game. Meta-narrative twist involving the Whispers.
media.notes: Intergrade (2021) adds Yuffie DLC chapter.

media.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
media.description: Part 2 of the Remake trilogy. Open world. Emotional and expansive. Widely praised.
media.notes: Part 3 (final entry of the trilogy) in development.

media.title: Final Fantasy Tactics
media.description: Tactical RPG set in Ivalice. Political fantasy epic directed by Yasumi Matsuno. One of the best tactical RPGs ever made.
media.notes: PSP version (War of the Lions) adds cutscenes and new jobs; recommended version.

media.title: Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles
media.description: Co-op action RPG. GBA link cable multiplayer was innovative for its time.
media.notes: Remaster (2020) available on PS4/Switch.

media.title: Final Fantasy Type-0
media.description: Dark, mature war story. Class Zero cadets at Akademeia. Originally Japan-only on PSP; HD release brought it West in 2015.

media.title: Dissidia Final Fantasy
media.description: 3D crossover fighting game featuring heroes and villains from FF I–XIII. Deep 1v1 combat.
media.notes: Dissidia 012 (2011) adds Lightning, Kain, Tifa, Laguna. NT (2018) divisive team-based revision.

media.title: Crisis Core: Final Fantasy VII
media.description: Zack Fair's prequel story. Essential FFVII lore. Crisis Core Reunion (2022) is the remaster; recommended version.
media.notes: Part of the FFVII Compilation.

media.title: Stranger of Paradise: Final Fantasy Origin
media.description: Action game prequel to the original Final Fantasy. Chaotic energy; cult following.
media.notes: Team Ninja collaboration. 'I want to kill Chaos' became a meme.

media.title: Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster I–VI
media.description: Definitive remasters of FF I–VI with updated sprites, new Uematsu music arrangements, and optional auto-battle.
media.notes: Released individually 2021; console versions added 2023.

media.title: Final Fantasy: Legend of the Crystals
media.description: OVA sequel set 200 years after Final Fantasy V. Non-canon; for completionists.

media.title: Final Fantasy: Unlimited
media.description: Original anime series with unique setting. Two children search for their parents. Uneven quality; incomplete ending.

media.title: Final Fantasy XV: Brotherhood
media.description: Anime exploring backstories of Noctis, Gladiolus, Ignis, and Prompto. Essential companion to FFXV. Free on YouTube and Crunchyroll.

media.title: Final Fantasy IX Animated Series
media.description: Animated adaptation of FFIX announced 2021 by French studio Cyber Group Studios. Status unclear as of 2025.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIV: Dad of Light
media.description: Live-action Japanese drama about a father and son bonding through playing FFXIV. Heartfelt and genuinely moving. Recommended for FFXIV players and non-fans alike.

media.title: Final Fantasy: The Spirits Within
media.description: First photorealistic CGI feature film. Sci-fi standalone universe, not connected to any game. Box office failure that contributed to Square's merger with Enix.
media.notes: Directed by Hironobu Sakaguchi.

media.title: Final Fantasy VII: Advent Children Complete
media.description: Extended director's cut of Advent Children. Adds ~30 min of new footage including extended Sephiroth fight. Definitive version.
media.notes: Original Advent Children released 2005. Complete is the recommended watch.

media.title: Kingsglaive: Final Fantasy XV
media.description: CGI prequel film set during the opening events of FFXV. Follows Nyx Ulric of the Kingsglaive. Watch before starting FFXV.
media.notes: Directed by Takeshi Nozue.

media.title: Final Fantasy VII: On the Way to a Smile
media.description: Short story collection bridging FFVII and Advent Children. Follows Cloud, Tifa, Barret, Yuffie, and Denzel. Official English translation available.
media.notes: Written by Kazushige Nojima. Canon FFVII Compilation.

media.title: Final Fantasy VII: The Kids Are Alright
media.description: Detective-style novel following Evan Townshend, an Aerith lookalike. Set two years post-FFVII. Highly regarded by the fan community. English translation available.
media.notes: Written by Kazushige Nojima. Canon.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIII: Episode Zero — Promise
media.description: Prequel novel covering events before FFXIII. Recommended for Lightning and Hope fans. English translation available.
media.notes: Written by Jun Eishima.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIII-2: Fragments Before
media.description: Prequel to FFXIII-2. Fills in the 500-year gap between the games.
media.notes: Written by Jun Eishima.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIII-2: Fragments After
media.description: Bridges FFXIII-2 and Lightning Returns.
media.notes: Written by Jun Eishima.

media.title: Final Fantasy XV: The Dawn of the Future
media.description: Novelization of the four canceled FFXV DLC episodes (Ardyn, Aranea, Lunafreya, Noctis alternate ending). Only way to experience the intended complete FFXV story. English translation available.
media.notes: Written by Jun Eishima. Essential for FFXV fans.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIV: Chronicles of Light
media.description: Short story anthology from FFXIV writers. Companion to A Realm Reborn era.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIV: Endwalker — The Art of Reflection: Histories Forsaken
media.description: Art and lore companion to the Endwalker expansion. Includes new short stories set in the Final Days era.

media.title: Final Fantasy X: Will
media.description: Sequel audio drama to FFX-2 bundled with the HD Remaster. Controversial for its implications for the ending. Sets up potential FFX-3.

media.title: Final Fantasy XV: Prologue — Parting Ways
media.description: Prequel audio drama. Noctis and Luna's last conversation before the events of FFXV.

media.title: Final Fantasy XV: Episode Aranea
media.description: Canceled DLC episode. Director Tabata departed Square Enix in 2018; three of four planned DLC episodes were canceled.
media.notes: Story partially covered in The Dawn of the Future novel.

media.title: Final Fantasy XV: Episode Lunafreya
media.description: Canceled DLC episode for Luna's perspective.
media.notes: Story covered in The Dawn of the Future novel.

media.title: Final Fantasy XV: Episode Noctis
media.description: Canceled DLC with alternate ending for Noctis.
media.notes: Story covered in The Dawn of the Future novel.

media.title: Final Fantasy XIV 1.0
media.description: Original FFXIV launch was critically panned as unfinished and poorly designed. Servers shut down November 2012. Rebuilt from scratch as A Realm Reborn.
media.notes: The in-game lore event 'The Calamity' served as the narrative bridge to ARR.
